stance_taxonomy.recognition: treats PA / PABs as a real, assessable phenomenon; operates or endorses PA-specific reunification / expert-witness work
stance_taxonomy.critique: argues PA construct is operationalised against DV/family-violence survivors; advocates DV/trauma-informed alternatives and tighter regulation
stance_taxonomy.middle: engages both alienating-behaviour and family-violence frames; differential-diagnosis approach aligned with FCFCOA family-report practice or AFCC-aligned models

australian_context_notes: Family Law Amendment Act 2023 (in force 6 May 2024) repealed s 61DA presumption of equal shared parental responsibility and rewrote s 60CC best-interests with safety primacy
FCFCOA controls the Family Report Writer pool (Reg 7 FLR 1984); s.121 Family Law Act 1975 anonymises individual FRWs in reported judgments
AHPRA / Psychology Board of Australia is the federal psychology register: https://www.psychologyboard.gov.au/Registration.aspx
PACFA and ACA are the self-regulatory bodies for counsellors/psychotherapists; AASW for social workers
Most active research lab: Family and Interpersonal Relationships Lab, UTAS (Matthewson) - first Australian PA research lab
Most active recognition NGO: Eeny Meeny Miney Mo Foundation (ACNC charity ID 4135fc80-3aaf-e811-a963-000d3ad244fd)
No Australian appellate-level Re Y [2026] EWFC 38 equivalent restricting unregulated PA experts

au_lawyers: Anne Hollonds AO (cross-listed here for psychologist credential)
ca_therapists: Lorri Yasenik PhD (ICCFL co-founder with Jon Graham Sydney)
us_therapists: William Bernet (PASG); Richard Warshak (Family Bridges, delivered in AU by Korosi, now withdrawn); Amy Baker; Linda Gottlieb

practitioners.name: Dr Mandy Matthewson, PhD, BA (Hons), MAPS, FCCLP
practitioners.practice: Senior Lecturer, School of Psychological Sciences, UTAS; Clinical Psychologist, Salamanca Psychology (Hobart); Lead, Family and Interpersonal Relationships Lab UTAS; Director, Eeny Meeny Miney Mo Foundation

practitioners.specialty: Clinical psychology; nature, impact and treatment of parental alienating behaviours (PABs); reunification post-alienation; adult alienated-children mental-health outcomes; targeted-parent psychotherapy

programs_or_publications: Family and Interpersonal Relationships Lab UTAS (first Australian PA research lab)
Haines, Matthewson & Beddoe (Routledge 2020) Understanding and Managing Parental Alienation
Verhaar, Matthewson & Bentley (2022) Children mental-health study
Director, Eeny Meeny Miney Mo Foundation
Past Chair PASG Research Committee; former Editor-in-Chief Parental Alienation International
practitioners.primary_contact: Mandy.Matthewson@utas.edu.au ; via Salamanca Psychology https://salamancapsychology.com.au/ ; 1st Floor, 8 Brooke Street, Hobart TAS 7000
practitioners.hook: Single most-cited Australian academic-clinician on PABs; UTAS lab and Routledge handbook are the load-bearing references in any recognition-camp AU conversation.
practitioners.notes: Dual academic + clinical role + NGO directorship + PASG leadership history makes her the linchpin of the Australian recognition camp.

Korosi (2016) Parental Alienation: Responding to Deliberate Ruptures (ResearchGate)

practitioners.notes: Personal lived experience as alienated father is part of public profile. PACFA (not AHPRA-psychology) status is the AU regulatory analogue of post-Re Y UK debate.

practitioners.name: Federal Circuit and Family Court of Australia - Family Report Writers Network (institutional)
practitioners.practice: Court-controlled pool of Family Report Writers (Reg 7 FLR 1984); Family Consultants employed by FCFCOA; private FRWs from Family Report Writers Network and equivalent panels
practitioners.regulatory_body: AHPRA Psychology Board OR AASW; FCFCOA Expert Witness Code of Conduct
practitioners.registration_number: individual FRWs not publicly enumerated due to s.121 Family Law Act 1975 anonymisation
practitioners.regulatory_notes: Court child experts and Panel Family Consultants must be fully AHPRA-registered psychologists OR AASW-eligible social workers, with minimum 5 years' experience with children and families (per FCFCOA published criteria)
practitioners.specialty: Court-ordered family reports under s.62G Family Law Act 1975; child interviews; observed family-relationship assessments; differential-diagnosis of parent-child contact problems (estrangement, alienation, justified rejection, mixed)
practitioners.stance: middle (institutional pool spans the spectrum; FCFCOA framework explicitly favours differential-diagnosis approach over presumptive PA finding; s.121 restricts naming individual FRWs)
practitioners.stance_source: FCFCOA Family Reports FAQ - explicit differential-diagnosis framework https://www.fcfcoa.gov.au/fl/pubs/family-reports ; Magellan list framework (CSA cases)
programs_or_publications: Magellan list (serious physical/sexual abuse allegations)
Section 11F / 62G family reports
Expert Witness Code of Conduct
Family Report Writers Network (private market) https://www.familyreportwriters.com.au/
practitioners.hook: Any AntiAlienate engagement with AU Family Court system runs through this institutional pool; FRW Network is the de facto AU middle camp by structural design. Treat as navigational signpost rather than outreach target.
practitioners.notes: Institutional not personal. Most-cited senior FRWs sitting under this pool include former FCFCOA Family Consultants whose names appear in non-anonymised academic / training contexts (e.g., Moloney - see Entry 9).

stance_distribution.note: Recognition: Matthewson, Korosi, Price-Tobler, Verhaar, Bentley, Graham (middle-leaning), EMMM institutional. Critique: Hollonds (critique-leaning middle), Humphreys. Middle: Moloney (middle-leaning critique), Smyth, FCFCOA FRW pool.

honest_gaps: AHPRA registration numbers are not publicly displayed for any psychologist entry - searchable but require name+DOB on https://www.psychologyboard.gov.au/Registration.aspx (not enumerable from public sources)
Family Report Writer named individuals are not safely enumerable due to s.121 Family Law Act 1975 anonymisation; institutional Entry 12 is the closest we can get without breach risk
PACFA/ACA registration numbers for Korosi and Price-Tobler not publicly displayed; verified by category (Clinical PACFA / MACA) only
Family Bridges accredited facilitator roster in Australia - no current public list; Korosi withdrew workshop 2024; status of other facilitators not publicly confirmed
Reconnect Family Program source-brief item could not be verified
EMMM Director Ms Fiona Blane - credentials, AHPRA/PACFA/AASW status, direct contact all not publicly confirmed; rolled into institutional Entry 7

Critique-camp clinical practitioners (as opposed to academics) are under-represented - AU DV/PA-critique discourse is primarily academic/policy (Humphreys, Hollonds, Rathus, Rhoades, Fehlberg) rather than clinical-practitioner-led; this is a structural feature of the AU field not a batch gap
Dr Daryl Higgins (ACU) deferred pending sourced PA-specific stance
